meta.title: How UK public money reaches health, education and the next generation
coverage: The UK revenue, pooled-fund, spending-control, parliamentary-authority, allocation and audit chain.
Top-level health and education institutions in England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland.
A deeper England map from departments through commissioners, local authorities and provider classes.
Comparable labels for planned, sought, authorised, allocated and outturn amounts without treating them as interchangeable.
Formal boards, committees, panels, public offices, functional contacts and non-residential public office locations.
Funding explicitly tagged for children, young people, learners, health-workforce training and research where the source supports that purpose.
exclusions: No claim that a particular taxpayer's payment funded a particular service unless legislation establishes that link.
No complete provider-level payment ledger, procurement join, school directory, NHS trust directory or award archive in this coverage-first release.
No bulk named-person roster, inferred biography, private contact, home address, staff dossier or applicant information.
No invented UK-wide 'next generation total': overlapping programmes, different years and incompatible budget boundaries are kept separate.
No medical, educational, legal or investment advice and no claim that a budget plan proves delivery or outcomes.
editorialRules: Most taxes are pooled. Follow authority and accounting records, not an imagined labelled pound.
A Spending Review plan, Estimate, appropriation, direction, allocation, contract, payment and audited outturn are different records.
Every amount carries a financial year, status, budget boundary, accounting basis, gross-or-net label and price basis.
Contained amounts are not added to their parents; not-comparable links remain visible.
Health and education are devolved. An England route is not silently presented as a UK route.
A board, advisory panel, accounting officer, commissioner, provider and auditor hold different powers.
Named current holders and experience histories remain on official source pages until a separate personal-data admission and correction process is approved.
Unknown, proposed, changing and not separately identifiable are valid findings.
meta.warning: Budget figures use different scopes and accounting boundaries. Read each record's status, containment and traceability warning before comparing or adding amounts.

institutions.name: HM Revenue & Customs
institutions.purpose: Administer taxes, National Insurance and specified recoveries, then account for those revenues separately from HMRC's own operating budget.
institutions.legalBasis: Commissioners for Revenue and Customs Act 2005 and tax-specific legislation; central-fund accounting follows the Exchequer and audit framework.
transparencyNotes: Collection does not earmark an ordinary tax receipt to a particular service.

institutions.name: HM Treasury
institutions.purpose: Set the spending-control framework, agree departmental limits, manage central funds and present most departmental Estimates to Parliament.
institutions.legalBasis: Treasury control derives from statute, annual Supply and long-standing constitutional practice described in the Supply Estimates manual.
transparencyNotes: A Treasury-approved budget is still distinct from parliamentary authority and departmental outturn.

evidence.locator: Role of HM Treasury and approval of Estimates

institutions.name: House of Commons
institutions.purpose: Vote the resource, capital, cash and purpose limits requested through Supply and scrutinise the use of public money through committees.
institutions.legalBasis: Parliamentary Supply and Appropriation Acts, House of Commons financial privilege and Standing Orders.
transparencyNotes: Parliament authorises limits and purposes; it does not administer provider payments.

evidence.locator: Supply, Main Estimates and Appropriation Acts

institutions.name: Office for Budget Responsibility
institutions.purpose: Produce independent economic and fiscal forecasts used to assess the likely path of receipts, spending, borrowing and debt.
institutions.legalBasis: Budget Responsibility and National Audit Act 2011 and the Charter for Budget Responsibility.
transparencyNotes: A forecast is neither a government allocation nor parliamentary spending authority.

institutions.name: National Audit Office
institutions.purpose: Support Parliament by authorising central-fund issues through the Comptroller function, auditing accounts and examining value for money.
institutions.legalBasis: Exchequer and Audit Departments Acts, National Audit Act 1983 and Budget Responsibility and National Audit Act 2011.
transparencyNotes: The NAO does not allocate health or education budgets and does not decide policy merits.

evidence.locator: NAO reports as basis for PAC value-for-money work

institutions.name: Department of Health and Social Care
institutions.purpose: Lead health and social-care policy in England, account to Parliament for the departmental group and direct or grant resources through distinct statutory routes.
institutions.legalBasis: Ministerial and departmental powers across NHS, public-health and social-care legislation, exercised within Supply and Treasury controls.
transparencyNotes: DHSC does not manage an individual's NHS treatment or local-authority care case.

evidence.locator: Departmental board remit and system leadership

institutions.name: NHS England
institutions.purpose: Lead the NHS in England, allocate resources to ICBs, directly commission specified services and exercise current statutory oversight and payment-scheme functions.
institutions.legalBasis: National Health Service Act 2006 and Health and Social Care Act 2012 as amended, pending any enacted and commenced 2026 Health Bill changes.
transparencyNotes: NHS England remained legally active on 10 July 2026; abolition was a Bill and transition programme, not completed fact.

evidence.locator: Proposed abolition and transfer, not yet completed

institutions.name: Integrated care boards in England
institutions.purpose: Plan and commission NHS services for defined populations, allocate local resources and work with councils and providers through each board's statutory duties.
institutions.legalBasis: National Health Service Act 2006 as amended by the Health and Care Act 2022 and current establishment orders.
transparencyNotes: There were 36 legal ICBs from 1 April 2026. Clusters remain separate legal entities unless and until formally changed.

evidence.locator: April 2026 ICB map, establishment and clustering explanation

institutions.name: NHS and contracted healthcare providers in England
institutions.purpose: Deliver NHS-funded services through NHS trusts, foundation trusts, primary-care contractors and other contracted provider forms.
institutions.legalBasis: Provider-specific statutory form, registration and contract; secondary-care payments follow the current NHS Payment Scheme where in scope.
transparencyNotes: Provider type, commissioner, contract, payment method and actual receipt must be joined rather than assumed.

institutions.name: Upper-tier, unitary and education local authorities in England
institutions.purpose: Exercise local statutory responsibilities, combine applicable grants and local revenues, and allocate or commission services through legally distinct routes.
institutions.legalBasis: Local government, education, public-health and social-care legislation plus annual grant determinations and finance regulations.
transparencyNotes: A class record is not one council. Funding powers and service responsibilities vary by authority type.

evidence.locator: Upper-tier and unitary public-health grant recipients
Council role in pooled health and care plans

institutions.name: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
institutions.purpose: Lead the English local-government finance framework and pay or coordinate grants that support local services and pooled health-and-care arrangements.
institutions.legalBasis: Departmental Supply plus local-government finance and grant legislation.
transparencyNotes: Unring-fenced local-government resources are not a programme-specific allocation once received.

evidence.locator: Joint national Better Care Fund framework and local-authority grant context

institutions.name: Department for Education
institutions.purpose: Lead children and education policy in England and allocate funding through local-authority, academy, provider, regulator and student-finance routes.
institutions.legalBasis: Education and children legislation, grant powers, contracts and annual Supply within Treasury controls.
transparencyNotes: The Education and Skills Funding Agency closed on 31 March 2025; its functions sit within DfE rather than a current separate body.

evidence.locator: Departmental group and funding classifications after ESFA closure

institutions.name: Department for Work and Pensions
institutions.purpose: From April 2026, own English adult-skills and apprenticeship policy and publish the applicable provider and employer funding rules.
institutions.legalBasis: Machinery-of-government allocation of ministerial functions plus apprenticeship and levy legislation.
transparencyNotes: Some 2026-27 payment administration remains with DfE on DWP's behalf; policy ownership and payment operation are not the same role.

institutions.name: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
institutions.purpose: Hold central science and research budgets, sponsor UKRI and set the strategic framework within which UKRI allocates funds.
institutions.legalBasis: Departmental Supply, the Higher Education and Research Act 2017 and the UKRI framework relationship.
transparencyNotes: A DSIT settlement is not a grant-award ledger and should not be added to the UKRI amount it contains.

evidence.locator: DSIT Main Estimate and UKRI budget relationship
Secretary of State appointment and accountability route

institutions.name: Maintained schools in England
institutions.purpose: Deliver state-funded school education under local-authority maintenance, delegated budgets and school governance.
institutions.legalBasis: Education Acts, School Standards and Framework Act 1998, annual school-finance regulations and local formula decisions.
transparencyNotes: This class excludes academies; an NFF illustration is not itself the final maintained-school budget.

evidence.locator: Local formula, delegated budget and recoupment boundary

institutions.name: Academy trusts in England
institutions.purpose: Operate one or more academies under funding agreements and receive direct grant funding from DfE after applicable recoupment.
institutions.legalBasis: Academies Act 2010, company and charity law, funding agreements and academy trust handbook requirements.
transparencyNotes: A trust is the accountable legal entity; an individual academy and the trust are not interchangeable.

evidence.locator: Academy grant and departmental consolidation context

institutions.name: Funded early-years providers in England
institutions.purpose: Deliver eligible early-years entitlement hours through provider types admitted by the applicable local authority and national rules.
institutions.legalBasis: Childcare legislation, early-years regulations, grant conditions and local provider agreements.
transparencyNotes: An hourly formula rate, authority allocation and provider payment are different values.

evidence.locator: Local-authority funding and provider pass-through guidance

institutions.name: Further-education and 16-to-19 providers in England
institutions.purpose: Deliver 16-to-19, further-education, skills and eligible high-needs provision under programme-specific funding rules.
institutions.legalBasis: Education and Skills Act 2008, provider funding agreements, grant conditions and annual formula guidance.
transparencyNotes: Financial years, academic years and learner-record reference periods must not be silently merged.

institutions.name: Office for Students
institutions.purpose: Regulate higher-education providers in England and distribute specified teaching, strategic-priority and capital grants.
institutions.legalBasis: Higher Education and Research Act 2017, statutory guidance and annual grant letters.
transparencyNotes: DfE may issue statutory guidance, but OfS regulatory case decisions and provider allocations follow the body's legal scheme.

institutions.name: Student Loans Company
institutions.purpose: Administer student-finance schemes on behalf of the four UK governments while the loan assets and policy remain with the relevant government.
institutions.legalBasis: Student-support legislation, company framework document and service arrangements with each government.

evidence.locator: SLC within the DfE group and loan-account boundary

institutions.name: UK Research and Innovation
institutions.purpose: Invest public funds in research and innovation through nine councils, cross-cutting programmes and defined award mechanisms.
institutions.legalBasis: Higher Education and Research Act 2017, DSIT framework and annual budget delegations.
transparencyNotes: A UKRI budget, council budget, award and paid claim are separate records; multi-year announcements may overlap annual totals.

institutions.name: Scottish Government
institutions.purpose: Set Scotland's budget within devolved competence and allocate resources to health boards, councils, education bodies and other public services.
institutions.legalBasis: Scotland Acts, annual Budget Acts and devolved tax and borrowing legislation.

Block grant and devolved adjustment boundary

institutions.name: NHS Scotland territorial and national boards

Education and Skills portfolio and support transition

offices.accountability: To the Authority Board, Department of Education and public-finance framework.

relationships.explanation: HMRC accounts for central taxes and pays receipts into the Exchequer's pooled fund under the applicable legal and accounting framework.
doesNotProve: This does not earmark an individual receipt for health, education or any provider.

evidence.locator: Trust Statement and Consolidated Fund treatment

relationships.explanation: The House of Commons grants Supply through the Parliamentary process; HM Treasury manages controls and cash within enacted authority.
doesNotProve: An Estimate or vote is not final outturn, and authority does not mean Treasury chooses every provider payment.

evidence.locator: Supply, Estimates, appropriation and Treasury-control framework

relationships.explanation: HM Treasury sets and monitors DHSC's departmental spending-control boundary within Parliamentary authority.
doesNotProve: A control total is not cash paid to NHS bodies or providers.

relationships.explanation: HM Treasury sets and monitors DfE's departmental spending-control boundary within Parliamentary authority.
doesNotProve: A control total is not a school, college or student payment.

relationships.explanation: HM Treasury calculates changes to Scotland's block grant under the published funding-policy framework.
doesNotProve: A Barnett consequential is not a ring-fenced Scottish health or education grant.

relationships.explanation: HM Treasury calculates changes to Wales's block grant under the published funding-policy framework.
doesNotProve: A Barnett consequential is not a ring-fenced Welsh health or education grant.

relationships.explanation: HM Treasury calculates changes to Northern Ireland's block grant under the published funding-policy framework.
doesNotProve: A Barnett consequential is not a ring-fenced Northern Ireland health or education grant.

relationships.explanation: DHSC issues statutory annual financial directions setting NHS England resource-use and capital limits.
doesNotProve: A directed limit is not cash, provider revenue or audited outturn.

relationships.explanation: NHS England publishes recurrent and programme allocations for integrated care boards using national approaches.
doesNotProve: An ICB allocation does not identify every service contract or payment.

relationships.explanation: ICBs arrange healthcare within their responsibilities and use applicable selection, contract and payment rules.
doesNotProve: Commissioning responsibility does not prove a particular provider won an award or was paid.

relationships.explanation: DHSC pays the ring-fenced public-health grant to eligible upper-tier and unitary local authorities.
doesNotProve: The grant total does not show a council's final service-by-service outturn.

relationships.explanation: ICBs contribute the required NHS amount to locally agreed Better Care Fund pooled budgets.
doesNotProve: The contribution is not additional to its source NHS allocation unless reconciled.

evidence.locator: Minimum NHS contribution and section 75 arrangements

relationships.explanation: Local authorities combine applicable grants and local contributions with NHS money in section 75 arrangements.
doesNotProve: Pooling does not transfer every statutory function to one partner.

relationships.explanation: DfE allocates Dedicated Schools Grant and other programme funding to local authorities under published formulae and conditions.
doesNotProve: A local-authority allocation is not every maintained school's final budget.

relationships.explanation: DfE funds academy trusts directly through grant agreements and programme-specific grants.
doesNotProve: A departmental grant does not prove an academy's final spend or outcome.

relationships.explanation: Local authorities set maintained-school budget shares through the lawful local formula.
doesNotProve: National or local notional formula figures are not automatically final outturn.

relationships.explanation: Local authorities pass the required share of early-years entitlement funding to eligible providers.
doesNotProve: A formula entitlement is not proof of attendance, invoice or outturn.

relationships.explanation: DfE issues eligible 16-to-19 and other education allocations to provider classes under published rules.
doesNotProve: A provider allocation needs matching period and boundary before comparison.

relationships.explanation: DfE sponsors the Office for Students and gives annual guidance for its grant functions.
doesNotProve: Sponsorship does not erase the regulator's statutory independence.

relationships.explanation: DfE is the principal policy and funding department for English student-finance delivery through SLC.
doesNotProve: Student-loan cash outlay is not provider grant or conventional expense.

relationships.explanation: DSIT sponsors and funds UKRI within the department's research and innovation boundary.
doesNotProve: The departmental line is not evidence of each award or recipient payment.

relationships.explanation: The Scottish Government allocates health resources to territorial and national NHS boards.
doesNotProve: The portfolio boundary is not directly comparable with other nations.

relationships.explanation: The Scottish Government provides local-government funding within which councils make education decisions.
doesNotProve: A settlement total is not a school-by-school allocation.

relationships.explanation: The Scottish Government funds SFC to allocate eligible college and university funding.
doesNotProve: The SFC total is not each institution's payment or outturn.

relationships.explanation: The Welsh Government allocates the Health and Social Care budget to NHS Wales bodies.
doesNotProve: The MEG total is not an NHS-only or provider-payment total.

relationships.explanation: The Welsh Government funds local authorities, after which local decisions shape school funding.
doesNotProve: The wider settlement does not expose one clean education-only total.

relationships.explanation: The Welsh Government funds Medr for tertiary education, research and related functions.
doesNotProve: Sponsorship does not prove each provider award.

relationships.explanation: Northern Ireland's Department of Health funds and commissions regional health and social-care bodies.
doesNotProve: The interim Vote on Account is not a settled full-year trust allocation.

relationships.explanation: The Department of Education funds and oversees the Education Authority for system delivery.
doesNotProve: Departmental control does not mean every school budget is decided centrally.

relationships.explanation: The Department for the Economy funds eligible further- and higher-education providers.
doesNotProve: A draft budget does not prove a final provider allocation.

relationships.explanation: The Comptroller and Auditor General audits departmental accounts and the NAO may examine value for money.
doesNotProve: Audit is not executive control or a finding about every transaction.

relationships.explanation: The Comptroller and Auditor General audits departmental accounts and the NAO may examine value for money.
doesNotProve: Audit is not executive control or a finding about every transaction.

relationships.explanation: PAC may examine accounts, accounting officers and value-for-money evidence concerning health spending.
doesNotProve: Scrutiny does not allocate budgets or manage services.

relationships.explanation: PAC may examine accounts, accounting officers and value-for-money evidence concerning education spending.
doesNotProve: Scrutiny does not allocate budgets or manage services.

relationships.explanation: HMRC collects National Insurance contributions, deducts the statutory NHS allocation and accounts for the remaining receipts in the Great Britain and Northern Ireland National Insurance Funds.
doesNotProve: The statutory NHS allocation does not trace an individual's contribution to a nation, hospital, service or appointment.

evidence.locator: Statutory background and NHS allocation notes

funds.name: UK Consolidated Fund
funds.purpose: Receive most central-government revenues and provide the statutory account from which authorised central-government cash issues are made.
inflows: Tax and other central-government receipts accounted for by revenue authorities.
Other receipts and transfers permitted by the Exchequer framework.
outflows: Cash issues supported by parliamentary Supply, Treasury control and Comptroller authorisation.
restrictions: A department still needs legal authority, an approved ambit and Treasury control before cash can be issued.
The pooled account does not label an ordinary tax receipt for a particular service.

evidence.locator: Consolidated Fund, Supply and cash-control sections
Trust Statement and Consolidated Fund revenue boundary

funds.name: Devolved government block grants
funds.purpose: Carry the UK-government block-grant settlement and later Barnett-related changes into each devolved government's overall funding position.
inflows: UK spending-review settlements and later changes calculated under the block-grant framework.
outflows: Overall block-grant funding available to the Scottish Government, Welsh Government and Northern Ireland Executive alongside their other financing.
restrictions: Barnett consequentials are not ring-fenced to the comparable English programme.
Devolved tax adjustments and other settlement features affect the final position.

funds.name: NHS England 2026-27 directed resource envelope
funds.purpose: Bound NHS England's resource and capital resource use under the annual financial directions before later allocations and commissioning decisions.
inflows: Resource-use limits directed by the Department of Health and Social Care.
outflows: ICB allocations, national commissioning and other NHS England functions within the directed limits.
restrictions: A resource-use limit is not a cash balance or proof of payment.
Specified sub-limits and exclusions remain part of the boundary.
notEquivalentTo: The narrower Spending Review NHS England RDEL line.

evidence.locator: Annex A1 and A2; resource and capital limits

funds.name: Better Care Fund local pooled budgets
funds.purpose: Pool specified NHS and local-government contributions under agreed local plans for integrated health, care and housing-related support.
outflows: Locally agreed schemes within an assured Better Care Fund plan and pooled-budget agreement.
restrictions: The national minimum is a composition of contributions, not wholly new spending.
A pool does not merge the participating legal bodies or transfer every statutory duty.

evidence.locator: Framework sections on section 75 pooling, planning and table 1 contributions

funds.name: Dedicated Schools Grant
funds.purpose: Carry the schools, central school services, high-needs and early-years blocks from DfE to local-authority funding systems.
inflows: DfE grant allocations calculated under the published block and formula arrangements.
outflows: Maintained-school delegated budgets, local central services, high-needs routes and early-years funding, subject to conditions.
Recouped amounts supporting direct academy grant routes.
restrictions: The grant is ring-fenced and its four blocks have distinct rules.
A local-authority allocation is not an individual school's final spend.

evidence.locator: Grant conditions, four-block structure and recoupment route
High-needs block, place funding and top-up route
Early-years block and provider pass-through route

funds.name: English student-finance cash outlay boundary
funds.purpose: Separate annual student-loan cash outlay and support administration from grant spending, loan-book valuation and later repayments.
inflows: Public financing made available for approved student support under the applicable schemes.
outflows: Approved tuition and maintenance support administered by the Student Loans Company.
restrictions: Loan outlay creates a financial asset and is not wholly equivalent to education expense.
Repayments, write-offs, subsidy estimates and carrying value use different measures.
notEquivalentTo: The resource accounting and budgeting charge.

evidence.locator: Forecast definitions for outlay, repayments, face value and RAB cost
Student-finance accounting boundary in consolidated accounts

funds.name: Great Britain and Northern Ireland National Insurance Funds
funds.purpose: Account for National Insurance contributions net of the statutory NHS allocation and finance contributory benefits under social-security law.
inflows: National Insurance contributions after HMRC deducts the statutory NHS allocation.
outflows: Other payments and transfers authorised by the governing legislation.
restrictions: The accounts are prepared on a receipts-and-payments basis under their statutory accounts directions.
The NHS allocation happens before contributions enter the funds and forms part of total NHS funding.

evidence.locator: Accounting policies, statutory background and NHS allocation notes

programmes.name: NHS services in England
programmes.purpose: Arrange and provide nationally and locally commissioned NHS services within current statutory responsibilities.
programmes.populationScope: All people eligible for NHS services; component services may target defined cohorts.
transparencyNotes: NHS England abolition is proposed, not yet an operative transfer of every function.
Child-specific spend is often not separately identifiable.

programmes.name: Local public-health services in England
programmes.purpose: Fund statutory and prescribed local public-health functions through a ring-fenced grant.
programmes.populationScope: Local populations, including children and adults; service mix varies within grant conditions.
transparencyNotes: The total is not a child-only budget.
Local outturn needs council-level returns.

programmes.name: Better Care Fund in England
programmes.purpose: Support locally planned integration of health, social care and housing-related support.
programmes.populationScope: Adults and older people are central, but local plans and prevention can reach wider groups.
transparencyNotes: Contributions overlap with source allocations and must not be double counted.
Local plans determine the precise service mix.

programmes.name: Core schools funding in England
programmes.purpose: Fund mainstream school provision through national and local formula routes and academy grants.
programmes.populationScope: Pupils in state-funded primary and secondary education.
transparencyNotes: National formula figures may be notional rather than final school budgets.
Maintained-school and academy periods can differ.

programmes.name: Funded early education and childcare entitlements
programmes.purpose: Fund eligible early-years entitlements through local-authority formulae and provider pass-through rules.
programmes.populationScope: Eligible children before compulsory school age and their families.
transparencyNotes: The 97% pass-through rule applies at the stated stream boundary, not to every council education pound.

programmes.name: High-needs and alternative-provision funding
programmes.purpose: Support special educational needs and disabilities, alternative provision, places and needs-led top-ups.
programmes.populationScope: Children and young people aged 0 to 25 within the published high-needs scope.
transparencyNotes: Core setting funding, place funding and top-up are distinct layers.
This is not a person-level entitlement ledger.

programmes.name: 16-to-19 education funding
programmes.purpose: Fund eligible 16-to-19 study programmes using provider allocation formulae and conditions.
programmes.populationScope: Young people in eligible school, college and other 16-to-19 provision.
transparencyNotes: Allocation statements can use an academic-year boundary and must not be silently added to financial-year totals.

programmes.name: Apprenticeships in England
programmes.purpose: Support employer-led apprenticeship training and assessment under levy and non-levy funding rules.
programmes.populationScope: Eligible apprentices and employers across age groups, including young people entering work.
transparencyNotes: Policy responsibility moved to DWP from April 2026 while parts of payment administration remain with DfE.
Levy receipts do not create an employer-owned cash pot.

programmes.name: Higher-education teaching and strategic priorities in England
programmes.purpose: Support eligible higher-education teaching, strategic priorities and capital through the Office for Students.
programmes.populationScope: Students and registered higher-education providers in England.
transparencyNotes: Grant funding is separate from tuition-fee loan cash and provider fee income.
Provider-level coverage is not yet enumerated.

programmes.name: Student finance for England
programmes.purpose: Deliver tuition-fee and maintenance loans and applicable support under English student-finance policy.
transparencyNotes: Loan cash outlay, face value, carrying value, subsidy and repayments are different measures.

programmes.name: UK research and innovation funding
programmes.purpose: Fund research, innovation, infrastructure, fellowships and knowledge exchange through UKRI and its councils.
programmes.populationScope: Researchers, innovators, institutions, businesses and future research workforces under individual schemes.
transparencyNotes: Awards may be grants, fellowships, contracts, loans or formula funding; they are not flattened into one instrument.

programmes.name: Health and social care portfolio in Scotland
programmes.purpose: Fund NHS Scotland and other health and social-care portfolio responsibilities under the Scottish budget.
programmes.populationScope: The Scottish population across health, care, prevention and workforce programmes.
transparencyNotes: The portfolio includes social care and other functions and is not directly comparable with an England NHS number.

programmes.name: Education and skills in Scotland
programmes.purpose: Fund schools, colleges, universities, skills and learner support through Scottish and local bodies.
programmes.populationScope: Children, learners, students, apprentices and adult learners in Scotland.
transparencyNotes: Several budget lines are grouped here; beneficiary tags do not create an additive total.

programmes.name: Health and Social Care Main Expenditure Group in Wales
programmes.purpose: Fund NHS Wales and other health and social-care responsibilities under the Welsh Final Budget.
programmes.populationScope: The Welsh population across health, care, prevention and workforce programmes.
transparencyNotes: The MEG is not NHS-only and is not directly comparable with another nation's differently bounded total.

programmes.name: Education and tertiary funding in Wales
programmes.purpose: Fund schools through local government and tertiary education, research and apprenticeships through Welsh bodies.
programmes.populationScope: Children, learners, students, apprentices and adult learners in Wales.
transparencyNotes: The reviewed budget does not expose one clean education-only amount across every route.

programmes.name: Health and social-care services in Northern Ireland
programmes.purpose: Fund and commission health, social care and related departmental services through Northern Ireland bodies.
programmes.populationScope: The Northern Ireland population across health, care, prevention and workforce programmes.
transparencyNotes: The Vote on Account is interim authority; the draft full-year amount is a plan.

programmes.name: Schools, further education and higher education in Northern Ireland
programmes.purpose: Fund grant-aided schools and eligible further- and higher-education provision through responsible bodies.
programmes.populationScope: Children, learners, students, apprentices and adult learners in Northern Ireland.
transparencyNotes: Education and Economy Department figures are separate boundaries.
Draft amounts are not final allocations.

fundingMechanisms.name: Parliamentary Supply and appropriation
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Government presents Estimates; the Commons grants Supply and legislation appropriates resources and cash to stated services.
conditions: Authority must be enacted or otherwise lawfully available.
Departments remain inside Treasury controls and accounting-officer duties.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: This is the legal-control spine before departmental allocation or payment.
doesNotProve: An Estimate is not enacted authority by itself.
Authority is not evidence that the full limit was spent.

fundingMechanisms.name: Devolved block-grant adjustment
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Published baseline and comparability rules calculate changes; each devolved government then sets its own budget.
conditions: The funding-policy rules and applicable fiscal frameworks govern calculation.
Portfolio choices remain devolved.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: Block-grant changes feed the overall devolved resource envelope, not a matched English programme.
doesNotProve: Barnett consequentials are not ring-fenced health or education grants.
This does not prove devolved outturn.

fundingMechanisms.name: DHSC financial directions to NHS England
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Annual directions set separate resource-use and capital limits and named conditions.
conditions: NHS England must comply with the stated statutory directions.
Resource and capital boundaries remain separate.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The direction constrains NHS England's use of resources before downstream allocations.
doesNotProve: The limit is not cash paid, provider income or outturn.

fundingMechanisms.name: NHS England allocations to integrated care boards
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: National formulae and policy adjustments produce recurrent and programme allocations for 36 current ICBs.
conditions: ICBs remain within allocation conditions and NHS financial rules.
Published workbooks and effective dates control the current values.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The allocation gives ICBs resources to arrange services within their responsibilities.
doesNotProve: It does not show every contract, invoice or provider payment.

fundingMechanisms.name: 2026/27 NHS Payment Scheme
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: National prices, local payment arrangements and rules apply according to service and transaction type.
conditions: Commissioner and provider must identify the applicable rule and contract.
Provider Selection Regime and Standard Contract duties remain separate.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The scheme describes how covered commissioner-provider activity is priced or paid.
doesNotProve: It is not the NHS budget, an allocation or evidence that a transaction occurred.

fundingMechanisms.name: Ring-fenced public-health grant
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: The Secretary of State determines council allocations and grant conditions for eligible public-health functions.
conditions: Recipients must comply with the ring fence and reporting conditions.
Local decisions must remain within statutory public-health responsibilities.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The grant transfers a bounded public-health resource to eligible councils.
doesNotProve: The grant total does not identify child-only spending or final local outturn.

fundingMechanisms.name: Better Care Fund pooled-budget mechanism
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: National minimum contributions and local plans are implemented through NHS Act section 75 pooled arrangements.
conditions: Each area needs an approved plan and local agreement.
Contributions retain their source and accounting context.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The mechanism coordinates local NHS and council resources for agreed integration schemes.
doesNotProve: The national sum is not additional to every contributing allocation.
Pooling does not prove outcomes.

fundingMechanisms.name: Dedicated Schools Grant
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: National calculations allocate schools, central school services, high-needs and early-years blocks to local authorities.
conditions: Block transfers and local formula decisions follow current rules.
Academy amounts are recouped for direct payment.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: DSG is the principal ring-fenced route into local school and high-needs funding.
doesNotProve: A local-authority DSG allocation is not each school's final budget.

fundingMechanisms.name: Academy General Annual Grant and related grants
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: DfE calculates and pays academy funding under funding agreements and applicable grant conditions.
conditions: Trustees and the accounting officer must meet academy financial requirements.
Academic-year and financial-year figures remain distinct.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: Direct academy grants replace the corresponding recouped local-authority route for academies.
doesNotProve: A grant is not evidence of trust outturn or school-level outcomes.

fundingMechanisms.name: Early-years national and local funding formulae
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: National rates feed local-authority allocations; local formulae and participation data determine provider funding.
conditions: At least the stated share of each entitlement stream must be passed through.
Eligibility and provider conditions apply.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The route connects entitlement funding to participating early-years providers.
doesNotProve: The pass-through percentage does not apply to unrelated council funding.
Formula funding is not proof of attendance or payment.

fundingMechanisms.name: High-needs place and top-up funding
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: The high-needs block supports place funding and locally agreed top-ups above core setting resources.
conditions: Core funding, place funding and top-up must be shown separately.
Commissioning and statutory assessment duties remain with the responsible bodies.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The mechanism funds provision for high-needs learners and alternative provision.
doesNotProve: It is not a child-level payment ledger or one uniform national price.

fundingMechanisms.name: 16-to-19 revenue funding formula
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Eligible learner numbers, programme factors and adjustments produce provider allocations.
conditions: Provider eligibility and funding-agreement conditions apply.
Academic-year boundaries must be retained.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: DfE issues provider allocation statements using the published formula.
doesNotProve: An academic-year provider allocation is not directly additive with departmental financial-year outturn.

fundingMechanisms.name: Apprenticeship service and funding rules
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Employer accounts, government support, funding bands and evidence rules govern training and assessment payments.
conditions: Current eligibility, evidence and funding-band rules apply.
Policy and payment-administration responsibilities are effective-dated.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The mechanism supports approved apprenticeship training and assessment.
doesNotProve: Levy paid is not an employer-owned fund or proof of provider payment.

fundingMechanisms.name: Office for Students recurrent and capital grants
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: DfE guidance sets the envelope and priorities; OfS determines eligible allocations under its powers.
conditions: Registered-provider and grant conditions apply.
Recurrent and capital funding remain separate.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The route supports teaching, strategic priorities and capital at eligible providers.
doesNotProve: It is not tuition-fee income or student-loan cash.

fundingMechanisms.name: Student-loan outlay and administration
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: SLC administers approved tuition and maintenance support under regulations and DfE policy.
conditions: Eligibility, entitlement and repayment rules apply.
Outlay and later valuation measures stay separate.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: Cash may pass to a student or provider while a public financial asset is recognised.
doesNotProve: Cash outlay is not wholly education expense, grant income or loan-book face value.

fundingMechanisms.name: UKRI council funding routes
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Councils use programme-specific formulae, peer review, competitions, fellowships, contracts or loans.
conditions: Each scheme's eligibility, assessment and award conditions apply.
Award instrument and period must be preserved.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: UKRI distributes research and innovation resources through nine councils and cross-cutting programmes.
doesNotProve: The UKRI budget line is not each award, payment or recipient outcome.

fundingMechanisms.name: Scottish budget and portfolio allocations
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Scottish ministers propose and Parliament authorises portfolios; the government then makes body and programme allocations.
conditions: Scottish statutory authority and controls apply.
Portfolio and body boundaries must be retained.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The mechanism turns Scotland's overall resources into Scottish policy choices.
doesNotProve: It is not a pass-through copy of England spending or a cross-nation comparator.

fundingMechanisms.name: Scottish Funding Council allocations
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: SFC publishes final college and university allocations using its sector-specific methods.
conditions: Academic-year and financial-year boundaries must not be mixed.
Institution and purpose conditions apply.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: SFC distributes Scottish tertiary funding to eligible institutions.
doesNotProve: A sector total is not each institution's cash or outturn.

fundingMechanisms.name: Welsh budget and body allocations
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Welsh ministers propose and the Senedd authorises budget groups before body and programme allocations.
conditions: Welsh statutory authority and controls apply.
MEG, local-government and body boundaries remain distinct.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The mechanism turns Wales's overall resources into Welsh policy choices.
doesNotProve: It is not a pass-through copy of England spending or a cross-nation comparator.

fundingMechanisms.name: Medr tertiary funding
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Medr allocates eligible tertiary, research and apprenticeship resources under Welsh policy and its statutory framework.
conditions: Provider, programme and grant conditions apply.
Different instruments and periods must remain visible.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: Medr is the national tertiary funding and regulation body in Wales.
doesNotProve: Governance status does not prove a particular award or payment.

fundingMechanisms.name: Northern Ireland budget authority and departmental allocations
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Assembly budget authority and Executive decisions provide departmental limits; current interim and draft states are kept separate.
conditions: Only enacted or otherwise lawful interim authority may be used.
Draft allocations stay labelled as plans.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The mechanism funds Northern Ireland departments and their delivery systems.
doesNotProve: A Vote on Account is not a settled full-year budget or provider allocation.

fundingMechanisms.name: Northern Ireland education delivery funding
fundingMechanisms.allocationMethod: Departments fund their remits; the Education Authority administers grant-aided school and system funding within delegated responsibilities.
conditions: Departmental, EA, school, FE and HE boundaries stay separate.
Final allocations require the applicable approved budget.
fundingMechanisms.explanation: The route distinguishes policy departments from the Education Authority and provider classes.
doesNotProve: Organisational structure alone does not prove a specific school or provider payment.

allocations.name: DHSC 2026-27 resource DEL plan
allocations.budgetBoundary: Spending Review 2025 DHSC RDEL in current prices
allocations.traceabilityWarning: A Spending Review plan is not enacted Supply, cash, NHS England income or outturn.

allocations.name: NHS England 2026-27 contained RDEL plan
allocations.budgetBoundary: Spending Review 2025 NHS England RDEL contained within DHSC
allocations.traceabilityWarning: This is contained within the DHSC plan and uses a narrower boundary than the statutory resource-use direction.

allocations.name: DHSC 2026-27 capital DEL plan
allocations.budgetBoundary: Spending Review 2025 DHSC CDEL in current prices
allocations.traceabilityWarning: Capital DEL is not resource spending, cash issued or outturn.

allocations.name: NHS England 2026-27 total resource-use limit

pipelineStages.name: Collect and account for revenue
pipelineStages.trigger: A tax or other central receipt becomes due and is collected.
pipelineStages.input: Tax and non-tax receipts under their own legal rules.
pipelineStages.output: Revenue accounted for in HMRC's Trust Statement and paid into the Exchequer as applicable.
pipelineStages.explanation: Collection establishes revenue, not a service allocation.
limits: Most receipts are pooled.
National Insurance has a separate statutory accounting route and a limited NHS allocation.

pipelineStages.name: Pool receipts and finance the aggregate cash position
pipelineStages.trigger: Receipts and lawful financing reach the central funds.
pipelineStages.input: Pooled receipts and public financing.
pipelineStages.output: An aggregate Exchequer cash position available only under legal authority.
pipelineStages.explanation: The pooled fund breaks any ordinary one-to-one trail from a tax receipt to a provider.
limits: Borrowing finances the overall gap rather than a named service.
Standing services and Supply use different legal authorities.

pipelineStages.name: Set fiscal and spending plans
pipelineStages.trigger: Government completes a Budget, Spending Review or later settlement decision.
pipelineStages.input: Forecasts, fiscal rules, policy choices and departmental bids.
pipelineStages.output: Planned departmental and devolved envelopes with stated periods and boundaries.
pipelineStages.explanation: A plan is not Parliamentary authority or outturn.
limits: Forecast error and later revisions remain possible.
Current prices, real prices, DEL and AME must remain distinct.

pipelineStages.name: Seek and grant Parliamentary Supply
pipelineStages.trigger: Government presents Estimates and the Commons completes the applicable Supply process.
pipelineStages.input: Departmental Estimate requests with resource, capital and cash limits.
pipelineStages.output: Enacted authority and appropriated services when the legislation completes.
pipelineStages.explanation: Parliament authorises limits; it does not execute every allocation or payment.
limits: A Main Estimate remains a request until the relevant legislation is enacted.
Standing services follow separate authority.

evidence.locator: Main Supply Estimates and pending status

pipelineStages.name: Control limits and issue cash
pipelineStages.trigger: A department needs to use authorised resources or draw cash.
pipelineStages.input: Enacted authority, Treasury controls and departmental cash forecasts.
pipelineStages.output: Controlled departmental resource and cash availability.
pipelineStages.explanation: Treasury control does not make Treasury the commissioner or provider.
limits: Cash and accrual resource are different.
The full authorised limit need not be used.

pipelineStages.name: Direct the NHS England resource boundary
pipelineStages.trigger: The financial year requires statutory DHSC directions.
pipelineStages.input: DHSC settlement, legal duties and annual directions.
pipelineStages.output: Separate NHS England resource-use and capital limits.
pipelineStages.explanation: The direction is a limit, not a provider payment.
limits: The boundary differs from the narrower Spending Review line.
Future abolition proposals do not transfer current functions automatically.

pipelineStages.name: Allocate resources to commissioners
pipelineStages.trigger: NHS England approves the current allocation workbooks and policy adjustments.
pipelineStages.input: National formula inputs, policy choices and the directed envelope.
pipelineStages.output: Recurrent and programme allocations to 36 ICBs and retained national functions.
pipelineStages.explanation: An allocation does not reveal every contract or invoice.
limits: Formula and convergence adjustments affect distribution.
Commissioning responsibilities vary by service.

pipelineStages.name: Agree pooled health and care plans
pipelineStages.trigger: ICBs and councils prepare locally agreed Better Care Fund plans.
pipelineStages.input: Minimum contributions, local priorities and section 75 governance.
pipelineStages.output: Approved local pooled arrangements and schemes.
pipelineStages.explanation: Pooling coordinates money but does not merge the bodies or every duty.
limits: Contributions overlap their source allocations.
Actual outcomes require later local evidence.

pipelineStages.name: Select, contract and price services
pipelineStages.trigger: A commissioner decides how to arrange a service within its legal responsibility.
pipelineStages.input: Population need, service specification, provider-selection rules and available allocation.
pipelineStages.output: A lawful contract and applicable payment method for the selected provider.
pipelineStages.explanation: Selection, contract and payment rules are distinct records.
limits: A contract is not proof of payment or good performance.
The payment scheme is not an allocation.

pipelineStages.name: Deliver and report health services
pipelineStages.trigger: A provider delivers commissioned or grant-funded activity.
pipelineStages.input: Contracts, grants, staff, assets and service demand.
pipelineStages.output: Activity, invoices, local returns and later accounts.
pipelineStages.explanation: Delivery evidence sits downstream of authority and allocation.
limits: This corpus does not contain patient records or a complete payment ledger.
Outturn follows later and uses provider-specific boundaries.

pipelineStages.name: Allocate departmental education programmes
pipelineStages.trigger: DfE applies grant, formula and programme rules within its settlement.
pipelineStages.input: Departmental authority, pupil and learner data, policy factors and grant conditions.
pipelineStages.output: Local-authority, academy and provider allocation decisions.
pipelineStages.explanation: Departmental totals and programme allocations may be contained rather than additive.
limits: Data updates and local formulae change final distribution.
Financial and academic years differ.

pipelineStages.name: Apply local formulae and needs decisions
pipelineStages.trigger: A local authority receives formula funding and completes required local decisions.
pipelineStages.input: Grant blocks, local data, formula consultation and individual commissioning duties.
pipelineStages.output: Maintained-school shares, provider funding, places and needs-led top-ups.
pipelineStages.explanation: Schools forums advise or decide only within their statutory remit; councils retain other responsibilities.
limits: Notional national figures are not final budgets.
Person-level needs records are outside this public corpus.

pipelineStages.name: Pay direct national and arm's-length routes
pipelineStages.trigger: A programme uses a direct academy, OfS, SLC, apprenticeship or UKRI route.
pipelineStages.input: Programme authority, eligibility, award method and delivery data.
pipelineStages.output: Grant, loan, training or research award under its own conditions.
pipelineStages.explanation: Different instruments stay separate even when they reach the same institution.
limits: A loan is not a grant.
An award is not necessarily a payment or outcome.

pipelineStages.name: Deliver education and report use
pipelineStages.trigger: A school, trust, provider or delivery body uses the funding under its conditions.
pipelineStages.input: Funding agreements, learner demand, staffing, places and local services.
pipelineStages.output: Teaching, support, returns and later accounts.
pipelineStages.explanation: Provider activity and outcomes need separate evidence from the allocation.
limits: This corpus does not expose pupil or learner records.
Provider accounts can use different periods.

pipelineStages.name: Adjust overall devolved funding
pipelineStages.trigger: UK fiscal decisions change comparable spending or another block-grant input.
pipelineStages.input: Published comparability, population and baseline rules.
pipelineStages.output: A change to each devolved government's overall resources.
pipelineStages.explanation: The change is not ring-fenced to the corresponding English programme.
limits: Fiscal frameworks and other adjustments also matter.
The resulting portfolio choice belongs to the devolved government.

pipelineStages.name: Choose devolved portfolios and obtain authority
pipelineStages.trigger: A devolved government prepares its budget under its legislature's process.
pipelineStages.input: Block grant, devolved revenue, permitted financing and policy choices.
pipelineStages.output: Authorised or clearly labelled draft portfolio budgets.
pipelineStages.explanation: Each nation has its own institutions, service boundary and budget state.
limits: Northern Ireland's reviewed full-year figures remained draft.
Cross-nation totals are not automatically comparable.

pipelineStages.name: Allocate to national and local delivery bodies
pipelineStages.trigger: A devolved portfolio budget is distributed through that nation's institutions.
pipelineStages.input: Approved portfolio budgets, formulae, grants and commissioning decisions.
pipelineStages.output: Body and provider allocations under nation-specific arrangements.
pipelineStages.explanation: The same institutional label can mean different powers in each nation.
limits: Local and provider completeness is not yet claimed.
Later accounts are needed for outturn.

pipelineStages.name: Prepare accounts and outturn returns
pipelineStages.trigger: The financial or reporting period closes.
pipelineStages.input: Ledgers, returns, consolidation rules and accounting policies.
pipelineStages.output: Departmental, group, body and provider accounts or official outturn statistics.
pipelineStages.explanation: Outturn is backward-looking and bound to the reporting perimeter.
limits: Audited accounts lag current plans.
Consolidation can remove internal transactions.

pipelineStages.name: Audit and examine value for money
pipelineStages.trigger: Auditable accounts or a selected public-spending question becomes available.
pipelineStages.input: Accounts, evidence and the auditor's statutory remit.
pipelineStages.output: Audit opinion, report or value-for-money study.
pipelineStages.explanation: Audit tests evidence and controls; it does not run the service.
limits: An audit opinion is not a guarantee about every transaction.
Value-for-money work does not decide policy merits.

pipelineStages.name: Scrutinise and require a public response
pipelineStages.trigger: A committee selects accounts, audit work or departmental policy for inquiry.
pipelineStages.input: Published accounts, audit reports, evidence and committee remit.
pipelineStages.output: Evidence sessions, reports, recommendations and government responses.
pipelineStages.explanation: Scrutiny makes responsibility visible but does not become executive management.
limits: Recommendations are generally not self-executing.
Committees do not determine criminal liability or individual complaints.

transparencyGaps.title: An ordinary tax pound cannot be traced to one service
transparencyGaps.detail: Most central receipts enter the Consolidated Fund before Parliament, Treasury and departments make later spending decisions.
transparencyGaps.consequence: The API can show authority and allocations, but must not create a taxpayer-to-school or taxpayer-to-hospital edge.

transparencyGaps.title: Four-nation health and education totals are not directly comparable
transparencyGaps.detail: Official budgets use different service boundaries, accounting presentations, stages and institutional structures.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Applications must show each boundary and must not rank nations from these headline figures as if they measured the same thing.

evidence.locator: Health and Social Care MEG boundary

transparencyGaps.title: No clean Wales education-only total in the reviewed budget source
transparencyGaps.detail: School funding sits partly inside local-government funding while tertiary and other routes use separate budget lines and bodies.
transparencyGaps.consequence: The coverage pass maps the route but does not publish a fabricated all-education total for Wales.

transparencyGaps.title: Northern Ireland full-year figures remained draft at review
transparencyGaps.detail: The reviewed multi-year budget source proposed departmental RDEL and CDEL values, while interim authority followed a separate process.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Draft allocations stay plans and must be replaced or effective-dated when a final budget is approved.

transparencyGaps.title: NHS England structural reform is not yet a completed transfer
transparencyGaps.detail: The Health Bill and transition material describe proposed abolition and later function changes, but current legal bodies and duties continue until the required law and commencement.
transparencyGaps.consequence: A future-state diagram must not overwrite current responsibility, allocations or accountability.

transparencyGaps.title: Current holders and committee members stay at official source links
transparencyGaps.detail: This first bulk corpus maps formal offices, board structures and roster doors without copying a changing people directory or personal biographies.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Readers can verify current holders at dated official pages; a future named-person layer needs a separate purpose, privacy, accuracy and correction review.

transparencyGaps.title: Provider contracts, payments, activity and outcomes are not yet one joined chain
transparencyGaps.detail: A commissioner allocation and payment rule do not identify every contract, invoice, recipient organisation or service result.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Do not infer that a provider received money without an exact official award, contract or payment record and stable identifier.

transparencyGaps.title: Child-specific health spending is often embedded in wider allocations
transparencyGaps.detail: NHS and public-health envelopes frequently serve all ages without a source-defined child-only split.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Beneficiary tags make relevant programmes findable but cannot support a made-up next-generation spending total.

evidence.locator: NHS planning envelope lacks child-only split

transparencyGaps.title: Next-generation beneficiary tags overlap
transparencyGaps.detail: A programme may serve several cohorts and one person may fit several cohort descriptions.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Never add tagged programme amounts unless an official total or non-overlapping calculation proves the boundary.

transparencyGaps.title: Academic-year and financial-year allocations need an official bridge
transparencyGaps.detail: Provider allocations can follow teaching cycles while departmental budgets and accounts follow 1 April to 31 March.
transparencyGaps.consequence: The API keeps period wording in the budget boundary and does not mechanically convert or add unlike periods.

transparencyGaps.title: Local bodies and providers are represented first as classes
transparencyGaps.detail: The coverage release does not enumerate every school, trust, health provider, council allocation, tertiary provider or local pooled scheme.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Class-level edges explain the system but do not prove a relationship or payment for a named organisation.

transparencyGaps.title: Current plans and authority run ahead of audited outturn
transparencyGaps.detail: Budgets, directions and allocations for 2026-27 are available before complete audited accounts for that period.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Users must not describe current plans as actual spend; outturn records should be added only after official publication.

transparencyGaps.title: Funding-specific formal power scores are not yet calibrated
transparencyGaps.detail: Office records expose responsibility, decision scope, constraints, appointment and accountability, but this corpus does not assign a second unreviewed game score.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Use the separate politics formal-office method and do not turn budget size, salary, fame or proximity into a person score.

transparencyGaps.title: Functional contact coverage is intentionally narrow
transparencyGaps.detail: Only reviewed shared mailboxes, switchboards and published departmental addresses enter the first release.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Absence of a contact does not mean a body is unreachable; follow the institution website and never infer staff email patterns.
